LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO FEB-18-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SET FORTH SPECIFIC FACTS SHOWING GOOD CAUSE FOR THE DISCOVERY. (CCP 2031.310(B)(1).) DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO A PROPERLY NOTICED MOTION. (CCP 2031.060(A).) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND LONDON BREED's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The City and County of San Francisco, its mayor, and two departments (collectively the City) demur to plaintiff Daniel Powell's Second Amended Complaint. The demurrer is sustained (see Word minutes for complete ruling).

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion; Motion To Compel Discovery; Declaration
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JOSE ZELIDON-ZEPEDA, Esq. of The City Attorney's Office (tel: 415-355-3312) for Defendants.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL's Motion To Compel Discovery.

The Court notes as of 9:13 A.M. the plaintiff / contesting party is not present. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Daniel Powell's motion to compel discovery is denied. Plaintiff failed to set forth specific facts showing good cause for the discovery. (CCP 2031.310(b)(1).) Defendants' request for a protective order is denied without prejudice to a properly noticed motion. (CCP 2031.060(a).)

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion, Motion To Disqualify, Motion To Sanction
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL's Motion To Disqualify, Motion To Sanction.

Plaintiff Daniel Powell's Motion To Disqualify and Motion To Sanction is DENIED.

Plaintiff seeks an order disqualifying deputy city attorney Jose Zelidon-Zepeda from representing Defendants in this action. Plaintiff has not shown a factual or legal basis for such order. Indeed, Penal Code section 1424 has no application to this civil case and there is no factual basis for removal pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 or section 128.7. The motion, thus, is denied.

Plaintiff seeks an order sanctioning counsel Zelidon-Zepeda. Plaintiff has not shown a factual or legal basis for such order. Penal Code section 1424 has no application to this civil case and there is no factual basis for sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 or section 128.7. The motion, thus, is denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1- DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND LONDON BREED'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer is SUSTAINED with 20 days leave to amend.

(see word minutes for full details)

A proposed order is submitted prior to the hearing for the court's review.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2- PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff Daniel Powell's motion to strike demurrer is DENIED.

Under CCP Section 436, the Court may "strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading," or "strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (CCP Section 436(a)(b).) CCP Section 435 states, "A notice of motion to strike a demurrer, or a portion thereof, shall set the hearing thereon concurrently with the hearing on the demurrer."

Plaintiff moves to "STRIKE this FRIVOLOUS demurrer from Delaying or Blocking Plaintiff's Righteous Action Herein, for any longer than procedurally necessary, hereafter." Plaintiff has not met his standard on the motion to strike to identify what irrelevant, false, or improper matter he seeks to have stricken. If the court assumes Plaintiff is moving to strike the entire demurrer, he does not provide any explanation, authority, or reasoning as to why the court should do that. Simply asserting the demurrer is frivolous does not meet the standard for a motion to strike. In any event, the court has found the demurrer meritorious, meaning Plaintiff's contention that it is frivolous has been rejected.

A proposed order is submitted prior to the hearing for the court's review.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion, Motion For Joinder, Declaration, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Dec-10-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court memorializes it received a contest email from Mr. Powell yesterday. Mr. Powell informed the clerk this morning that he was not appearing to contest the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as folows:

PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL's Motion For Joinder.

Plaintiff Daniel Powell's Motion for Joinder is DENIED. The motion is based on CCP 379, which establishes a substantive rule governing when persons may be joined as defendants in a single action. CCP 379, however, is not a vehicle for adding parties as defendants. A plaintiff seeking to add a party as a defendant must follow the procedure for filing an amended complaint. (See CCP 473.) Whether the complaint is sufficient as to any claim or named defendant will turn on a review of the allegations and claims stated in the complaint. Here, Plaintiff has not submitted an amended complaint or proposed amended complaint and, therefore, there is no basis to determine whether the Police Department, Sheriff's Department or any other party may properly be added as a defendant.

Counsel for the prevailing party to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301)
